stories.headline: Tensions mark first pretrial conference in Vice President Duterte's Senate impeachment
stories.summary: The House prosecution panel and Vice President Sara Duterte's defense team held their first closed-door pretrial conference Thursday, with both sides reporting heated disputes over procedural matters despite describing the session as productive. The prosecution plans to call 57 witnesses while the defense indicated 25 to 30; by day's end, both parties had largely agreed on evidence for the third article covering bribery and graft charges. Further pretrial sessions are scheduled for Monday, June 22, with a target of completing pretrial by Tuesday.
stories.context: Duterte faces four articles of impeachment covering alleged misuse of P612.5 million in confidential funds, unexplained wealth, bribery linked to Department of Education officials, and conspiracy involving threats against government officials.
stories.watch_for: Pretrial resumes Monday, June 22, with both sides targeting completion by Tuesday; the full Senate trial is set to begin July 6. Senate President Gatchalian indicated he would preside unless senators decide otherwise.

stories.headline: Puerto Galera declares calamity over water shortage, orders price freeze on essential goods
stories.summary: The municipality of Puerto Galera in Oriental Mindoro enacted a price freeze on basic necessities after its local council approved a state of calamity declaration due to a severe water shortage. Under the Price Act, prices of essential goods including canned fish, bottled water, instant noodles, and bread are automatically frozen at current levels during declared calamities for a maximum of 60 days. Businesses violating the controls face fines of P5,000 to P1 million, imprisonment of one to ten years, or both.

stories.headline: Bicol police major arrested for allegedly extorting P38,000 from PNP exam applicant in Albay
stories.summary: A police major assigned to the Bicol police regional medical unit was arrested in an entrapment operation in Legazpi City after allegedly demanding P38,000 from a Philippine National Police applicant in exchange for facilitating passage through the medical examination stage of the recruitment process. The operation was conducted by the Regional Intelligence Unit 5 and the Legazpi City Police Station following intelligence reports and surveillance. PNP chief Gen. Jose Melencio Nartatez Jr. said the case will be pursued through proper legal and administrative processes.

stories.headline: DPWH inaugurates 57 km of Samar-Eastern Samar highway, cutting travel time between Tacloban and Borongan by half
stories.summary: The Department of Public Works and Highways inaugurated 57 of the planned 70 kilometers of the Basey-Maydolong Road connecting Samar and Eastern Samar provinces, reducing travel time between Tacloban and Borongan cities from four hours to approximately two hours. The P3.1-billion project provides an alternative route during heavy rainfall when existing roads become impassable due to flooding and landslides. The remaining 13 kilometers are targeted for completion before President Marcos's term ends.

stories.headline: Doctors and lawyers challenge Ombudsman ruling clearing officials in P60-billion PhilHealth fund transfer
stories.summary: A coalition of doctors and lawyers filed a motion for reconsideration with the Office of the Ombudsman, contesting its June 2 resolution dismissing plunder and graft charges against then-Finance Secretary Ralph Recto and former PhilHealth president Emmanuel Ledesma Jr. over a P60-billion transfer from PhilHealth reserves. Complainants allege investigators did not examine who benefited from the diversion, citing a rise in infrastructure contracts in Recto's Batangas district from P50 million in 2023 to P3.6 billion in 2024. Recto has described the case as a harassment suit.

stories.headline: PDP-Laban formally recognizes Gatchalian's election as Senate president after 13-vote majority
stories.summary: The Partido Demokratiko ng Pilipinas Laban officially recognized Senator Sherwin Gatchalian as Senate president, following his election with 13 votes from chamber members. Senator Robin Padilla confirmed the recognition, stating the outcome aligned with the Constitution and PDP-Laban's position, and denied allegations that personal interests guided his voting.
stories.context: Gatchalian was elected Senate president on June 18, succeeding outgoing Senate president Francis Escudero amid realignments in the chamber's political composition.

stories.headline: Marcos and Putin agree to expand energy and food cooperation; ASEAN and Russia adopt Kazan Declaration
stories.summary: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and Russian President Vladimir Putin held bilateral talks on the sidelines of the 35th ASEAN-Russia Commemorative Summit in Kazan, agreeing to explore broader cooperation in energy, food security, trade, and investment as both nations mark 50 years of diplomatic relations. The summit produced the Kazan Declaration 2026 and an ASEAN-Russia Comprehensive Plan of Action for 2026 to 2030, with Russia pledging continued exports of food, energy, and fertilizers to Southeast Asia. Putin noted that bilateral Philippine-Russian trade has surpassed $500 million annually.
stories.context: The Philippines holds the ASEAN chairmanship in 2026. This is Marcos's first visit to Russia since taking office, following his father Ferdinand Marcos Sr.'s 1976 Moscow trip that established the two countries' diplomatic relations.
stories.watch_for: Marcos has invited Putin to attend the 21st East Asia Summit in Manila in November 2026.

stories.headline: Putin agrees to release 24 Filipinos detained nine months in Siberia after Marcos raises case at ASEAN summit
stories.summary: President Marcos announced that Russian President Vladimir Putin has authorized the release of 24 Filipino nationals held for nine months in Irkutsk, Siberia, on immigration violations linked to illegal recruitment. Marcos raised the case during their bilateral meeting at the ASEAN-Russia Summit in Kazan, and Putin pledged the deportation would proceed by Friday. The detained Filipinos had not been charged with any offense during their time in detention.

stories.headline: DOE anticipates major fuel price rollback next week after US-Iran ceasefire memorandum signed
stories.summary: The Department of Energy announced that fuel prices are expected to drop substantially next week, with the Oil Industry Management Bureau citing observed declines in international crude oil prices following the US-Iran ceasefire memorandum. DOE bureau director Rino Abad said retail fuel prices are anticipated to fall to the P70 to P80 per liter range, though still above the pre-conflict level of P50 to P60 per liter. The DOE said it will continue monitoring the situation for sustained price stabilization.
stories.context: The Strait of Hormuz, which handles roughly 20% of global oil and LNG exports, was closed during the US-Iran conflict, pushing Philippine fuel prices sharply higher.
stories.watch_for: The DOE will monitor whether price reductions are sustained as the 60-day US-Iran compliance and negotiating period progresses.

stories.headline: Pag-IBIG reduces home loan rates to as low as 4.5% annually to boost affordable housing access
stories.summary: The Home Development Mutual Fund announced a promotional reduction in home loan interest rates, setting a 4.5% annual fixed rate for the first three years on properties valued up to P2.5 million and 5.75% for loans from P2.5 million to P10 million, down from the previous floor of 6.25%. Pag-IBIG chief executive Marilene Acosta said the lower rates aim to reduce monthly payments and help member-families build savings during the initial loan period, after which rates will be repriced according to borrower preference.

stories.headline: Easterlies bring isolated rain showers over Luzon and Visayas; distant tropical depression not a threat
stories.summary: PAGASA reported that easterlies will keep skies partly cloudy to cloudy over Central Luzon, Southern Luzon, and Visayas on Friday, with isolated rain showers and thunderstorms expected across most of the country. A tropical depression located approximately 2,110 kilometers east of Eastern Visayas poses no immediate threat to the Philippines. PAGASA warned of possible flash floods and landslides in vulnerable areas during severe thunderstorms.

stories.headline: Police seize P950,000 in expired food from Quiapo vendors; five arrested for tampering with expiry dates
stories.summary: National Capital Region police seized approximately P950,000 worth of expired food and merchandise from vendors near Villalobos and Palanca streets in Barangay 386, Quiapo, Manila, with officers finding that expiration dates on some items had been altered or tampered with. Five vendors were arrested and face charges under the Consumer Act and the Food Safety Act before the Manila City Prosecutor's Office. Three devices allegedly used to tamper with expiration markings were also confiscated.

stories.headline: US and Iran sign ceasefire memorandum; oil tankers begin transiting Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed a memorandum of understanding to end their war and open a 60-day negotiating period, with the United States lifting its naval blockade of Iranian ports as an immediate step. At least six oil tankers have since transited the Strait of Hormuz, and crude oil prices fell below $74 per barrel for the first time since early March. Trump stated the deal is signed but cautioned he could resume attacks if the agreement is violated.
stories.context: The Strait of Hormuz handles roughly 20% of global oil and liquefied natural gas exports. Its closure during the US-Iran conflict caused significant disruption to global energy markets and drove up fuel prices worldwide.
stories.watch_for: A 14-point agreement was released by the US; the 60-day compliance and negotiating period will determine whether a lasting settlement emerges.

stories.headline: Ukraine strikes Moscow oil refinery for second time in a week as Zelenskyy seeks G7 support
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones struck a Moscow oil refinery for the second time in a week, triggering fires and prompting Russia to temporarily suspend flights at Sheremetyevo airport; Russia said it intercepted 555 Ukrainian drones in a single overnight operation. President Volodymyr Zelenskyy held talks with US President Trump, French President Macron, and G7 leaders, securing pledges for additional air defense missiles and expanded sanctions on Russia's energy sector. The UK announced plans to deliver 150,000 drones to Ukraine by year's end.

stories.headline: Cuba's Communist Party approves sweeping free-market economic reforms amid deepening crisis
stories.summary: Cuba's Communist Party authorized an emergency economic package introducing free-market elements unprecedented for the island, including expanded private enterprise, foreign investment from diaspora Cubans, private real estate development, conversion of state enterprises into private ventures, and private banking. President Miguel Diaz-Canel acknowledged that domestic factors including bureaucracy contributed to the crisis and said the situation demands urgent changes. The National Assembly is expected to approve the package imminently.
stories.context: Cuba's economy has deteriorated under intensified US pressure during the Trump administration, including blocked fuel deliveries, alongside the decades-long US trade embargo.

stories.headline: UN records highest-ever child rights violations in armed conflicts; government forces named top perpetrators for first time
stories.summary: A United Nations report documented nearly 25,000 children experiencing a record 38,558 violations in armed conflicts last year, the highest figures in three decades of monitoring. For the first time, government forces rather than armed groups were identified as the primary perpetrators, with Israeli military forces accounting for 12,445 documented cases; Congo, Myanmar, and Somalia also recorded over 2,000 violations each. UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres called on Israel to develop a concrete plan to address the violations.
